scope.summary: The Local Integrator Rig scaffold provides the rig.sh command namespace, Tier A pseudo-home isolation, report skeleton, host-hygiene assertion, and boundary lint wiring.
out_of_scope: External MCP client reachability over stdio or HTTP; those are R1/R2/R3 follow-ups.
Browser automation lanes and installed-artifact sweeps beyond the scaffold command shell.
Tier B invasive systemd/container lanes beyond the non-invasive Tier A default.
Any sibling rust-oracledb driver source change.

entry_points.name: bash scripts/rig/rig.sh run --dry-run --log
entry_points.trace: Passed on 2026-07-22. The rig doctor reported 16 passing preflight checks, rig-boundary-lint passed, Tier A pseudo-home preparation was scheduled, L1 xe18/xe21/free23 setup/assert/teardown stages were scheduled, report generation passed, and host hygiene was explicitly dry-run skipped.

entry_points.name: bash scripts/rig/rig.sh report --dry-run --log && bash scripts/rig/rig.sh down --dry-run --log
entry_points.trace: Passed on 2026-07-22. The report command emitted a findings.md path under target/e2e/rig, and the down command exercised the safe teardown refusal/allowance path in dry-run mode.

entry_points.name: bash -n scripts/rig/rig.sh scripts/rig/rig_boundary_lint.sh scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh && bash scripts/rig/rig_boundary_lint.sh
entry_points.trace: Passed on 2026-07-22; shell syntax is valid and the committed boundary lint reported rig-boundary-lint: OK.

limitations: The final verification is scaffold-level and dry-run for the host-mutating paths; no full workspace Cargo gate is claimed from this shared dirty checkout.
The default Tier A scaffold is closed here; richer external-client and browser coverage remains in dependent rig beads.
Dirty files outside the evidence scope remain in the shared checkout and are excluded from tree_clean by the close auditor.
